analiza: Arnaldi already beat Dzumhur 6–3 6–4 on clay and comes in as a solid favourite around 1.48–1.50 – better clay stats, better H2H, I’m backing Arnaldi to win
Davidovich Fokina is the more explosive clay player and the market’s pick at roughly 1.50–1.66, while Molcan is priced near 2.20 – I’m siding with Davidovich Fokina to take this.

Both England and Argentina have scored in every knockout match so far, with their games regularly going over 3 total goals. With elite attacks on both sides and a very balanced 1X2 market, a cagey 0–0 looks unlikely.

Over 39.5 games looks like the best way to play this final: Sinner is a clear favourite, but Zverev has the serve, pedigree and five‑set experience to keep this close. With most realistic scripts pointing to at least four sets rather than a quick 3‑0, a long, high‑quality battle fits better than a blowout scoreline

mecz: Muchová vs Nosková – Muchová to win
analiza: Muchova comes into this final with a very solid tournament profile, handling long rallies well and managing the tempo in big moments. Noskova has had a strong run, but over a longer match Muchova looks like the more stable player with a broader toolkit, so backing her to win makes sense at this price, even if it still sits in the risky bracket

Norway are a tricky opponent, but across the tournament England have shown more depth, a better balance between attack and defence, and stronger game management in knockout football. With the odds around 1.85–1.95 offering a bit of value over simple qualification, the most likely scripts are narrow England wins in 90 minutes, which makes England to win in regular time a reasonable, but still risky, angle rather than any kind of banker.

Third-place games can be emotional, but the line of over 3.5 goals looks high here. Both teams are coming off painful semi-final defeats, the managers are expected to rotate, and France will be without William Saliba; multiple changes can reduce rhythm and cohesion.
